title: Emirates NBD (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Emirates NBD in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Emirates NBD?
faq.answer: Emirates NBD is a leading private sector bank operating in Egypt. It is the Egyptian subsidiary of Emirates NBD Group, one of the largest banking groups in the Middle East and North Africa region. The bank serves over 200,000 retail customers and more than 3,000 corporate clients across Egypt, offering comprehensive banking services through its extensive branch network. Emirates NBD Egypt is positioned as a market challenger in the Egyptian banking sector, currently ranking as the eighth-largest private sector bank in the country. The institution combines local market expertise with the regional strength and knowledge of its parent company in the UAE.

faq.question: When was Emirates NBD founded?
faq.answer: Emirates NBD Egypt was originally established on May 12, 1977, as Banque du Caire et de Paris, a joint venture between Egypt's Banque du Caire and Banque Nationale de Paris (BNP). The bank operated under BNP Paribas ownership for several decades following the evolution of its French parent company. On December 20, 2012, Emirates NBD Group announced the acquisition of BNP Paribas Egypt S.A.E. for USD 500 million. The transaction was completed in June 2013 after receiving approval from the Central Bank of Egypt and other regulatory authorities. This acquisition represented Emirates NBD's first major international expansion and strategic entry into the Egyptian market, bringing together BNP Paribas Egypt's local expertise with Emirates NBD's regional strength.

faq.question: Where is Emirates NBD headquartered?
faq.answer: Emirates NBD Egypt is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt's capital city. The bank maintains a significant presence in Cairo with numerous branches throughout the metropolitan area, including locations in prominent districts such as Downtown, Maadi, Zamalek, Heliopolis, Nasr City, and Garden City.

faq.question: Where does Emirates NBD operate?
faq.answer: Emirates NBD Egypt operates across all major regions of Egypt with a network of over 67 branches and units. The bank's geographic coverage spans Greater Cairo, Giza, Alexandria, the North Coast, the Delta region, Upper Egypt, Sinai, and the Red Sea. Major operational hubs include Cairo's business districts, Alexandria's commercial areas, resort destinations such as Sharm El Sheikh and Hurghada, and emerging development zones including New Cairo, 6th of October City, Sheikh Zayed, and Obour City. This widespread presence enables the bank to serve customers in both urban centers and developing economic zones throughout Egypt.

faq.question: What services does Emirates NBD offer?
faq.answer: Emirates NBD Egypt provides a comprehensive range of banking services across three major business segments: Retail Banking: Personal accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit, time deposits, personal loans, credit cards, debit cards, and youth banking services. The bank offers specialized products such as the Current Plus Account and Daily Saving Account. Corporate Banking: Commercial lending, trade finance, cash management, treasury services, and corporate solutions for businesses of all sizes. The bank has a particular focus on supporting small and medium enterprises (SMEs). Investment Solutions: Wealth management, investment products including money market funds (such as the Emirates NBD Money Market Fund "Mazid"), fixed income funds, and brokerage services. Digital Banking Services: Emirates NBD Egypt offers mobile banking through its dedicated mobile app, online banking platforms, and the smartTRADE web portal for trade services. The mobile app features instant transfers up to EGP 3 million, biometric authentication (fingerprint and Face ID), self-registration capabilities, bill payments, and account management. Digital channels allow customers to perform over 200 services with instant processing.

faq.question: What makes Emirates NBD unique?
faq.answer: Emirates NBD Egypt distinguishes itself through several key characteristics: Rapid Growth Trajectory: Since its acquisition in 2013, the bank has grown from the 11th-largest private sector bank to the 8th position, outpacing industry growth rates and demonstrating strong market momentum. Digital Innovation Focus: The bank launched online and mobile banking platforms in 2015 and has continuously expanded its digital offerings. It emphasizes financial inclusion through technology, aligning with the Central Bank of Egypt's priorities for expanding access to banking services. International Connectivity: Emirates NBD Egypt leverages its parent company's global network spanning 13 countries, providing customers with enhanced capabilities for international trade, foreign direct investment facilitation, and cross-border transactions. Strategic Partnerships: Following the acquisition, Emirates NBD maintained cooperative relationships with BNP Paribas in areas such as trade finance, cash management, and corporate investment banking. The bank has also secured innovative financing, including being the first Egyptian bank to receive a subordinated loan from the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) in 2017. Chinese Investment Focus: The bank has positioned itself to capture Chinese foreign direct investment in Egypt, including opening a dedicated office in the North-East Suez Canal industrial zone and establishing Yuan Nostro account capabilities.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Emirates NBD?
faq.answer: Emirates NBD Egypt is a commercial bank operating as a joint stock company (S.A.E. - Société Anonyme Egyptienne) under Egyptian law. It is classified as a universal bank, providing both retail and corporate banking services alongside investment banking capabilities. As a commercial bank, it operates under the supervision of the Central Bank of Egypt and is licensed to accept deposits, extend credit, and offer a full spectrum of financial services to individuals, corporations, governments, and financial institutions. The bank's current employee base exceeds 2,000 staff members with extensive experience in both local and global markets.
